questions.question: Which of the following is a primary function of an operating system?
options: Executing application code directly on the CPU
Managing hardware resources and providing a user interface
Designing graphic user interfaces for software
Providing electrical power to system components
questions.correctAnswer: Managing hardware resources and providing a user interface
questions.Help: The OS acts as an intermediary between hardware and user applications.

questions.question: What is the purpose of virtual memory in an operating system?
options: To store frequently accessed data for faster retrieval
To provide a permanent storage location for critical system files
To allow a system to compensate for shortages of physical memory by temporarily transferring data to disk storage
To encrypt sensitive data stored on the hard drive
questions.correctAnswer: To allow a system to compensate for shortages of physical memory by temporarily transferring data to disk storage
questions.Help: Virtual memory extends RAM by using disk space.

questions.question: Which file system is primarily used by modern Windows operating systems for its advanced security and file size support?
questions.correctAnswer: NTFS
questions.Help: NTFS offers features like journaling, security, and large file support.

questions.question: A user is experiencing an application crash on Windows 10, but the application ran fine on Windows 7. Which feature should they try to use?
questions.correctAnswer: Compatibility Mode
questions.Help: Compatibility mode helps older software run on newer OS versions.

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Power-On Self-Test (POST) during the boot process?
options: To load the operating system kernel into memory
To configure network settings and connect to the internet
To initialize and test hardware components before the OS loads
To display the login screen for user authentication
questions.correctAnswer: To initialize and test hardware components before the OS loads
questions.Help: POST is an initial diagnostic check.

questions.question: A computer repeatedly displays a 'Blue Screen of Death' (BSOD). Which of the following is the MOST likely cause?
options: Malfunctioning RAM or device driver issue
questions.correctAnswer: Malfunctioning RAM or device driver issue
questions.Help: BSODs often point to critical hardware or driver failures.

questions.question: Which Windows utility is used to free up disk space by deleting temporary files, Recycle Bin contents, and other non-essential items?
questions.correctAnswer: Disk Cleanup
questions.Help: This utility targets unnecessary files for removal.

questions.question: When considering an operating system upgrade, what is a crucial first step to ensure a smooth transition?
options: Backing up all important data and files
questions.correctAnswer: Backing up all important data and files
questions.Help: Data loss is a major risk during OS upgrades.

questions.question: Which Linux command is used to display the contents of a file?
questions.correctAnswer: cat
questions.Help: This command concatenates and displays file content.

questions.question: An administrator needs to quickly find the IP address of a Windows computer from the command prompt. Which command should they use?
questions.correctAnswer: ipconfig
questions.Help: This command displays current TCP/IP network configuration values.

questions.question: Which component of an operating system manages the allocation of CPU time to different processes?
questions.correctAnswer: Kernel
questions.Help: The kernel is the core of the OS, managing system resources.

questions.question: What characteristic makes ExFAT a suitable file system for large external storage devices like USB drives and SD cards?
options: Its compatibility with both Windows and macOS and support for large files
Its optimized performance for internal SSDs
questions.correctAnswer: Its compatibility with both Windows and macOS and support for large files
questions.Help: ExFAT bridges the gap between FAT32 and NTFS for cross-platform compatibility.

questions.question: Before installing new software, it is best practice to create a system restore point. What is the primary benefit of this action?
options: It backs up all user documents.
It allows the system to revert to a previous working state if issues arise.
questions.correctAnswer: It allows the system to revert to a previous working state if issues arise.
questions.Help: System Restore helps recover from problematic installations.

questions.question: If a Windows computer fails to boot and presents options like 'Safe Mode,' 'Last Known Good Configuration,' or 'Start Windows Normally,' which boot method is being utilized?
questions.correctAnswer: Recovery Environment
questions.Help: These options are typically part of the Windows Recovery Environment.

questions.question: A user reports that their computer is running extremely slowly and the hard drive activity light is constantly on, even when no applications are open. Which OS utility would be most helpful in identifying processes consuming excessive resources?
questions.correctAnswer: System Monitor (Task Manager on Windows)
questions.Help: Task Manager or similar system monitors show real-time resource usage.

questions.question: When upgrading an operating system, why is checking hardware compatibility an important consideration?
options: To ensure the new OS can utilize cloud storage efficiently
To verify that all existing peripherals and internal components will function correctly with the new OS
To reduce the power consumption of the system
To prevent the new OS from connecting to public Wi-Fi networks
questions.correctAnswer: To verify that all existing peripherals and internal components will function correctly with the new OS
questions.Help: Older hardware might lack drivers or support for newer OS versions.

questions.question: Which command-line tool in Linux is used to create a new directory?
questions.correctAnswer: mkdir
questions.Help: This command means 'make directory'.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Windows Registry?
options: To store user documents and personal files
To manage network connections and protocols
To store configuration settings and options for the operating system and installed applications
To handle printer queues and print jobs
questions.correctAnswer: To store configuration settings and options for the operating system and installed applications
questions.Help: The Registry is a hierarchical database for system settings.

questions.question: A user needs to enable file compression on a folder in Windows to save disk space. Where would they typically find this option?
options: In the 'Properties' dialog box of the folder
questions.correctAnswer: In the 'Properties' dialog box of the folder
questions.Help: File compression is a property of files and folders.

questions.question: If a computer is stuck in an endless reboot loop and cannot load the OS, which boot method might allow an IT professional to diagnose and potentially resolve the issue by starting the system with minimal drivers?
questions.correctAnswer: Safe Mode
questions.Help: Safe Mode loads only essential drivers and services, helpful for troubleshooting.

questions.question: Which protocol is responsible for logically addressing devices on a network and enabling routing between different networks?
questions.correctAnswer: IP address
questions.Help: IP addresses are used for logical addressing and routing.

questions.question: What type of network typically covers a small geographical area, such as a home, office, or school?
questions.correctAnswer: LAN
questions.Help: LAN stands for Local Area Network.

questions.question: Which protocol provides reliable, ordered, and error-checked delivery of a stream of octets between applications running on hosts communicating over an IP network?
questions.correctAnswer: TCP
questions.Help: TCP is a connection-oriented, reliable protocol.

questions.question: Which IP addressing scheme automatically assigns IP addresses to devices on a network?
questions.correctAnswer: Dynamic IP (DHCP)
questions.Help: DHCP servers assign dynamic IP addresses.

questions.question: Which IEEE standard defines the specifications for Wireless Local Area Networks (WLANs)?
questions.Help: 802.11 is the family of Wi-Fi standards.

questions.question: What is the maximum theoretical speed for Cat 5e Ethernet cable over typical distances?
questions.correctAnswer: 1 Gbps
questions.Help: Cat 5e supports Gigabit Ethernet.

questions.question: Which network service translates human-readable domain names (like www.example.com) into IP addresses?
questions.correctAnswer: DNS
questions.Help: DNS is the Domain Name System.

questions.question: A device that connects multiple computers within a local area network (LAN) and filters traffic by sending data only to the specific port that the destination device is connected to is called a:
questions.correctAnswer: Switch
questions.Help: Switches operate at Layer 2 and are more intelligent than hubs.

questions.question: How do MAC addresses primarily facilitate communication on a local network?
options: By providing logical addresses for routing across the internet
By identifying network devices uniquely at the data link layer
By encrypting data packets before transmission
By assigning dynamic IP addresses to new devices
questions.correctAnswer: By identifying network devices uniquely at the data link layer
questions.Help: MAC addresses are hardware addresses for local network identification.

questions.question: Which type of network typically spans large geographical areas and connects multiple smaller networks?
questions.correctAnswer: WAN
questions.Help: WAN stands for Wide Area Network.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the UDP protocol?
options: To guarantee reliable and ordered packet delivery
To provide a connectionless, unreliable, but fast data transmission
To establish secure connections for web browsing
To translate IP addresses to MAC addresses
questions.correctAnswer: To provide a connectionless, unreliable, but fast data transmission
questions.Help: UDP is often used for streaming where speed is prioritized over guaranteed delivery.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a private IP address?
options: To allow direct access to a device from the internet
To identify a device uniquely across the global internet
To be used for communication within a local network and are not routable on the public internet
To provide a fixed IP address that never changes
questions.correctAnswer: To be used for communication within a local network and are not routable on the public internet
questions.Help: Private IPs are for internal network use.

questions.question: Which wireless encryption standard is considered the most secure for Wi-Fi networks today?
questions.correctAnswer: WPA3
questions.Help: WPA3 is the latest and most robust Wi-Fi Protected Access standard.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of fiber optic cables over copper Ethernet cables?
options: Resistance to electromagnetic interference and longer transmission distances
Ability to carry electrical power to devices
questions.correctAnswer: Resistance to electromagnetic interference and longer transmission distances
questions.Help: Fiber optic uses light signals, making it immune to EMI and capable of greater distances.

questions.question: A network device that modulates and demodulates signals to convert digital data into analog signals for transmission over telephone lines or cable lines is called a:
questions.correctAnswer: Modem
questions.Help: Modems connect your network to the ISP's network.

questions.question: Which of the following describes an IPv6 address?
questions.correctAnswer: A 128-bit hexadecimal address
questions.Help: IPv6 addresses are significantly longer than IPv4.

questions.question: When a computer connects to a network, what is the role of DHCP?
options: To convert private IP addresses to public IP addresses
To assign unique hardware addresses to network interfaces
To dynamically assign IP addresses and other network configuration parameters to devices
To secure network traffic using encryption
questions.correctAnswer: To dynamically assign IP addresses and other network configuration parameters to devices
questions.Help: DHCP simplifies network configuration for clients.

questions.question: Which protocol is commonly used for secure remote command-line access to network devices?
questions.correctAnswer: SSH
questions.Help: SSH (Secure Shell) encrypts the connection, unlike Telnet.

questions.question: A user wants to extend their home Wi-Fi network's range. What type of device would be most suitable?
options: A wireless access point or repeater
questions.correctAnswer: A wireless access point or repeater
questions.Help: These devices specifically boost or extend Wi-Fi signals.

questions.question: In the context of networking, what is a MAC address also known as?
questions.correctAnswer: Hardware address
questions.Help: MAC addresses are burned into the network interface card.

questions.question: Which component is often referred to as the 'brain' of the computer, responsible for executing instructions and performing calculations?
questions.correctAnswer: CPU
questions.Help: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

questions.question: A user wants to improve the graphical performance of their desktop computer for gaming. Which expansion card should they consider upgrading?
questions.correctAnswer: Graphics card (GPU)
questions.Help: GPUs are specialized for rendering graphics.

questions.question: When purchasing a new computer, what is a key consideration if the user intends to perform demanding tasks like video editing or CAD?
options: A powerful multi-core CPU and ample RAM
questions.correctAnswer: A powerful multi-core CPU and ample RAM
questions.Help: Demanding tasks require significant processing power and memory.

questions.question: Which type of storage device uses non-volatile flash memory and offers significantly faster read/write speeds compared to traditional hard disk drives?
questions.correctAnswer: SSD
questions.Help: SSD stands for Solid State Drive.

questions.question: Which type of cable is commonly used to connect an analog telephone to a wall jack?
questions.correctAnswer: RJ11
questions.Help: RJ11 connectors are smaller and typically used for telephone lines.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using a fiber optic network cable over a Cat 6 Ethernet cable for long distances?
options: It is immune to electromagnetic interference
It carries electrical power for devices
It is more flexible and easier to bend
questions.correctAnswer: It is immune to electromagnetic interference
questions.Help: Fiber optic cables transmit data using light pulses, not electrical signals.

questions.question: After installing a new RAM module, the computer fails to boot and emits a series of beeps. What is the MOST likely immediate step in troubleshooting?
options: Check if the RAM module is seated correctly in its slot
questions.correctAnswer: Check if the RAM module is seated correctly in its slot
questions.Help: Beep codes often indicate a problem with RAM or other critical components.

questions.question: If a computer powers on, but the display remains blank and the internal fans spin at maximum speed, what might be the cause?
options: A software bug in the operating system
An issue with the video card or monitor connection
questions.correctAnswer: An issue with the video card or monitor connection
questions.Help: No display combined with active fans often points to video output problems.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the motherboard in a computer system?
options: To store all permanent data and files
To act as the main circuit board connecting all components and allowing them to communicate
To provide power to all internal components
questions.correctAnswer: To act as the main circuit board connecting all components and allowing them to communicate
questions.Help: The motherboard is the central hub for all hardware.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a Network Interface Card (NIC)?
options: To display video output on a monitor
To provide wireless connectivity to peripherals
To enable a computer to connect to a network
questions.correctAnswer: To enable a computer to connect to a network
questions.Help: A NIC provides the physical connection for network communication.

questions.question: Which of the following is a critical factor to consider when upgrading a power supply unit (PSU)?
options: The color of the PSU's casing
The total wattage required by all system components
The number of USB ports on the front panel
The brand of the operating system
questions.correctAnswer: The total wattage required by all system components
questions.Help: An underpowered PSU can cause system instability or prevent components from working.

questions.question: Which USB standard introduced a reversible connector and significantly increased data transfer speeds compared to previous versions?
questions.correctAnswer: USB-C (often associated with USB 3.1/3.2/Thunderbolt)
questions.Help: USB-C is a versatile and modern connector.

questions.question: A user notices their computer frequently freezes and restarts unexpectedly. Which component, if failing, could be a common cause of such instability?
questions.correctAnswer: Power Supply Unit (PSU)
questions.Help: An unstable or failing PSU can cause random reboots and crashes.

questions.question: What is the main function of RAM (Random Access Memory) in a computer?
options: To permanently store the operating system
To provide temporary storage for data and programs currently in use by the CPU
To process graphical information for display
questions.correctAnswer: To provide temporary storage for data and programs currently in use by the CPU
questions.Help: RAM is volatile memory for active data.

questions.question: Which cable type is most commonly used to connect a computer to a modern high-definition monitor or television?
questions.correctAnswer: HDMI
questions.Help: HDMI transmits both video and audio digitally.

questions.question: A new internal hard drive is not detected by the operating system after installation. What should be checked first?
options: The power and data cables are securely connected to the drive and motherboard
questions.correctAnswer: The power and data cables are securely connected to the drive and motherboard
questions.Help: Physical connections are crucial for detection.

questions.question: What does 'GPU' stand for, and what is its primary role?
options: General Processing Unit, for everyday tasks
Graphics Processing Unit, for rendering images and video
Gigabit Power Unit, for power delivery
Global Positioning Unit, for location services
questions.correctAnswer: Graphics Processing Unit, for rendering images and video
questions.Help: GPUs are specialized processors for visual data.

questions.question: Which expansion slot type is primarily used for high-performance graphics cards today?
questions.correctAnswer: PCIe
questions.Help: PCIe (PCI Express) is the current standard for expansion cards.

questions.question: A desktop computer suddenly shuts down without warning. After restarting, it immediately shuts down again. What is a common hardware issue that could cause this?
options: Overheating due to a failing CPU fan or clogged heatsink
questions.correctAnswer: Overheating due to a failing CPU fan or clogged heatsink
questions.Help: Thermal shutdown is a protective measure against overheating.

questions.question: Which type of network cable is typically used for older cable television and internet connections?
questions.correctAnswer: Coaxial
questions.Help: Coaxial cable is common for cable TV and older broadband.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of 'access control' in computer security?
options: To monitor network traffic for suspicious activity
To ensure only authorized users can access specific resources
To encrypt all data stored on a computer
To recover lost or corrupted files
questions.correctAnswer: To ensure only authorized users can access specific resources
questions.Help: Access control dictates who can do what with resources.

questions.question: Which type of malicious software is designed to collect personal information or browsing habits without the user's knowledge?
questions.correctAnswer: Spyware
questions.Help: Spyware is focused on surveillance.

questions.question: What is a firewall primarily designed to do?
options: Detect and remove viruses from a system
Encrypt network traffic to prevent eavesdropping
Monitor and control incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predefined security rules
Backup system data to an external location
questions.correctAnswer: Monitor and control incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predefined security rules
questions.Help: Firewalls act as a barrier between a trusted and untrusted network.

questions.question: What is 'social engineering' in the context of cybersecurity?
options: Using artificial intelligence to detect cyber threats
The psychological manipulation of people into performing actions or divulging confidential information
Developing secure software through collaborative coding
questions.correctAnswer: The psychological manipulation of people into performing actions or divulging confidential information
questions.Help: Social engineering exploits human psychology, not technical vulnerabilities.

questions.question: Which type of cyber attack involves overwhelming a system, service, or network with traffic to disrupt its normal operations?
questions.correctAnswer: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS)
questions.Help: DDoS attacks aim to make a service unavailable.

questions.question: What is 'spoofing' in cybersecurity?
options: The process of cleaning a computer's physical components
The act of disguising communication from an unknown source as being from a known, trusted source
A method of backing up data to multiple locations
An advanced form of data encryption
questions.correctAnswer: The act of disguising communication from an unknown source as being from a known, trusted source
questions.Help: Spoofing attempts to deceive by faking identity.

questions.question: In the CIA triad of information security, what does 'Confidentiality' refer to?
options: Ensuring data is accurate and has not been tampered with
Ensuring data is available to authorized users when needed
Protecting information from unauthorized access and disclosure
Logging all system events for auditing purposes
questions.correctAnswer: Protecting information from unauthorized access and disclosure
questions.Help: Confidentiality prevents unauthorized eyes from seeing data.

questions.question: What is a 'zero-day' vulnerability?
options: A software bug that has been patched for over a year
A vulnerability that is known to the public and the vendor, and a patch is available
A newly discovered software vulnerability for which no patch or fix has been released by the vendor
A security flaw found on the first day of a software's release
questions.correctAnswer: A newly discovered software vulnerability for which no patch or fix has been released by the vendor
questions.Help: Zero-day means the vendor has 'zero days' to fix it.

questions.question: Which version of Wi-Fi Protected Access (WPA) introduced an individualized encryption key for each wireless client?
questions.correctAnswer: WPA2
questions.Help: WPA2 significantly improved security over WPA.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an internal security threat?
options: A hacker launching a DDoS attack from a foreign country
A natural disaster destroying a data center
An employee accidentally clicking on a phishing link and compromising network credentials
A worm exploiting a software vulnerability
questions.correctAnswer: An employee accidentally clicking on a phishing link and compromising network credentials
questions.Help: Internal threats originate from within the organization.

questions.question: What is a common purpose of 'auditing' and 'event logging' in security?
options: To encrypt all user data automatically
To provide a chronological record of system activities for security analysis and forensic investigation
questions.correctAnswer: To provide a chronological record of system activities for security analysis and forensic investigation
questions.Help: Logs are crucial for detecting and investigating security incidents.

questions.question: An email purporting to be from a bank asks the recipient to click a link and verify their account information. This is an example of what type of cyber attack?
questions.correctAnswer: Phishing
questions.Help: Phishing attempts to trick users into revealing sensitive information.

questions.question: What is the main goal of applying the principle of 'least privilege' in security permissions?
options: To grant all users administrator access for convenience
To ensure users and processes have only the minimum necessary access to perform their legitimate functions
To encrypt all communication channels by default
To eliminate the need for passwords
questions.correctAnswer: To ensure users and processes have only the minimum necessary access to perform their legitimate functions
questions.Help: Least privilege reduces the impact of a security breach.

questions.question: Which concept in the CIA triad ensures that data is consistent, accurate, and trustworthy, and has not been improperly modified?
questions.correctAnswer: Integrity
questions.Help: Integrity focuses on the correctness and completeness of data.

questions.question: Why is it important to keep antivirus software up-to-date?
options: To ensure it can detect the latest emerging threats and malware definitions
To extend the battery life of a laptop
To reduce the physical wear and tear on hard drives
questions.correctAnswer: To ensure it can detect the latest emerging threats and malware definitions
questions.Help: New malware appears constantly, requiring updated definitions.

questions.question: Which preventive care measure is crucial for extending the lifespan of a laptop battery?
options: Always keeping the laptop plugged in to a charger
Fully discharging the battery frequently before recharging
Avoiding extreme temperatures and regularly exercising the battery (charging/discharging within healthy ranges)
Using a high-wattage universal charger for faster charging
questions.correctAnswer: Avoiding extreme temperatures and regularly exercising the battery (charging/discharging within healthy ranges)
questions.Help: Lithium-ion batteries are sensitive to heat and benefit from proper usage cycles.

questions.question: Compared to desktop computers, what is a common hardware difference in laptops regarding their power supply?
options: Laptops typically use larger, internal power supply units with higher wattage.
Laptops primarily use external AC adapters and have internal batteries, while desktops have internal PSUs.
Laptops do not require a power supply, running solely on battery.
Laptops use only DC power directly from the wall.
questions.correctAnswer: Laptops primarily use external AC adapters and have internal batteries, while desktops have internal PSUs.
questions.Help: Laptop power management is optimized for portability.

questions.question: A user wants to ensure their photos and contacts are accessible across their smartphone, tablet, and laptop. What is the most common method for cross-device data synchronization?
options: Manual USB transfer for all devices
Saving data only to local device storage
Utilizing cloud storage and synchronization services
questions.correctAnswer: Utilizing cloud storage and synchronization services
questions.Help: Cloud services offer seamless syncing across multiple devices.

questions.question: What is a key limitation of Bluetooth technology compared to Wi-Fi for data transfer?
options: It generally has a shorter range and lower data transfer speeds.
It is incompatible with mobile devices.
questions.correctAnswer: It generally has a shorter range and lower data transfer speeds.
questions.Help: Bluetooth is designed for short-range personal area networks.

questions.question: A laptop user reports that their Wi-Fi connection is constantly dropping. What is a common troubleshooting step for this issue?
options: Updating the operating system to a newer version
Restarting the wireless router and the laptop's Wi-Fi adapter
questions.correctAnswer: Restarting the wireless router and the laptop's Wi-Fi adapter
questions.Help: Many connectivity issues can be resolved with a simple restart of devices.

questions.question: Which wireless connectivity method allows a mobile phone to share its cellular data connection with other devices, effectively turning it into a Wi-Fi hotspot?
questions.correctAnswer: Mobile Hotspot/Tethering
questions.Help: This feature creates a personal Wi-Fi network from cellular data.

questions.question: A laptop is running slowly, and the user wants to improve its performance. What is one of the most common and effective component upgrades for a laptop?
options: Upgrading the CPU to a desktop-grade processor
Replacing the existing RAM with more or faster RAM
Adding an external graphics card via USB
Replacing the motherboard with a larger one
questions.correctAnswer: Replacing the existing RAM with more or faster RAM
questions.Help: Increasing RAM often significantly improves multitasking and overall speed.

questions.question: If a laptop's charging port feels loose and the battery only charges intermittently, what is the most likely hardware issue?
options: An issue with the LCD screen
A damaged charging port (DC jack) on the motherboard
questions.correctAnswer: A damaged charging port (DC jack) on the motherboard
questions.Help: Loose or damaged charging ports are common points of failure.

questions.question: Which of the following describes a key difference in RAM usage between mobile devices (smartphones/tablets) and desktop computers?
options: Mobile devices typically use more RAM than desktops.
Mobile devices often integrate RAM directly into the SoC (System on a Chip) and generally have less user-upgradable RAM than desktops.
Mobile devices use a different type of volatile memory entirely.
Desktop RAM is designed for lower power consumption than mobile RAM.
questions.correctAnswer: Mobile devices often integrate RAM directly into the SoC (System on a Chip) and generally have less user-upgradable RAM than desktops.
questions.Help: Space and power efficiency are critical in mobile design.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of cleaning a laptop's cooling vents regularly?
options: To prevent dust buildup, which can cause overheating and reduce performance
To extend the life of the keyboard
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent dust buildup, which can cause overheating and reduce performance
questions.Help: Good airflow is essential for thermal management.

questions.question: A user's mobile phone is unable to connect to any cellular network, even though other phones in the area have service. What is a probable troubleshooting step?
options: Performing a factory reset on the phone
Checking if Airplane Mode is enabled or if the SIM card is properly inserted
Connecting the phone to a Wi-Fi network
questions.correctAnswer: Checking if Airplane Mode is enabled or if the SIM card is properly inserted
questions.Help: These are basic checks for cellular connectivity.

questions.question: What is NFC (Near Field Communication) primarily used for in mobile devices?
options: Streaming high-definition video to a TV
Short-range wireless communication for payments, data exchange, or pairing
Providing a cellular data connection to other devices
questions.correctAnswer: Short-range wireless communication for payments, data exchange, or pairing
questions.Help: NFC works over very short distances, typically a few centimeters.

questions.question: Which part of a laptop is typically *not* user-replaceable or upgradable due to its integrated nature?
questions.correctAnswer: CPU (on most modern laptops)
questions.Help: Most modern laptop CPUs are soldered directly to the motherboard.

questions.question: A tablet's screen is unresponsive to touch, but the power button still works. What might be a temporary fix or troubleshooting step?
options: Connecting an external mouse via USB
Performing a forced restart or hard reset of the device
Plugging it into a different charger
questions.correctAnswer: Performing a forced restart or hard reset of the device
questions.Help: A software glitch can often be resolved by restarting the device.

questions.question: What is a characteristic of 5G wireless connectivity that makes it advantageous for mobile devices over previous generations?
options: Significantly higher speeds and lower latency
Only compatible with older mobile devices
questions.correctAnswer: Significantly higher speeds and lower latency
questions.Help: 5G is designed for next-generation mobile broadband performance.

questions.question: Which consumable is essential for an inkjet printer to produce images and text?
questions.correctAnswer: Ink cartridge
questions.Help: Inkjet printers use liquid ink.

questions.question: Which port type is most commonly used to connect modern printers to a computer?
questions.correctAnswer: USB Port
questions.Help: USB is the universal standard for peripheral connection.

questions.question: A printer is connected to a computer, but it is not responding to print commands. What is the FIRST step in troubleshooting?
options: Checking if the printer is powered on and properly connected, and if there are any error messages on the printer display
questions.correctAnswer: Checking if the printer is powered on and properly connected, and if there are any error messages on the printer display
questions.Help: Always start with the most basic checks.

questions.question: If an inkjet printer is producing faded or blank pages, what is a common cause?
options: The ink cartridges are low or clogged
questions.correctAnswer: The ink cartridges are low or clogged
questions.Help: Lack of ink or blocked nozzles lead to poor print quality.

questions.question: What is a key maintenance task for a laser printer?
options: Replacing ink cartridges every few weeks
Replacing the toner cartridge and occasionally cleaning residual toner/dust
questions.correctAnswer: Replacing the toner cartridge and occasionally cleaning residual toner/dust
questions.Help: Laser printers use toner and have different maintenance needs than inkjet.

questions.question: A small office wants to allow multiple computers to print to a single printer without connecting it directly to each PC. What is the most common method for achieving this?
options: Connecting the printer to a single PC and sharing it locally (PC host sharing)
Using individual USB cables for each computer
Connecting the printer directly to the network via Ethernet or Wi-Fi (network printer)
Using a Bluetooth adapter for each computer
questions.correctAnswer: Connecting the printer directly to the network via Ethernet or Wi-Fi (network printer)
questions.Help: Network printers are designed for shared access.

questions.question: Which print setting allows a user to print on both sides of a sheet of paper automatically?
questions.correctAnswer: Duplex
questions.Help: Duplex printing refers to two-sided printing.

questions.question: What is a common characteristic of thermal printers?
options: They use liquid ink and produce high-quality color photos.
They use toner powder and a fuser to create images.
They use heat to create images on specially coated paper and often do not require ink or toner.
They connect to computers primarily via parallel ports.
questions.correctAnswer: They use heat to create images on specially coated paper and often do not require ink or toner.
questions.Help: Thermal printers are common for receipts and labels.

questions.question: Which troubleshooting step should be performed if a network printer is 'not found' by client computers?
options: Check the printer's network configuration (IP address) and connectivity (cables/Wi-Fi)
Update the client computer's video drivers
questions.correctAnswer: Check the printer's network configuration (IP address) and connectivity (cables/Wi-Fi)
questions.Help: Network issues are often about addressing or physical connection.

questions.question: To save paper, a user wants to print multiple pages onto a single sheet. What print preference would allow them to do this?
questions.correctAnswer: Pages Per Sheet (N-up printing)
questions.Help: This setting reduces the number of physical pages used.
